chapter_title.en: The Dislike of Sleeping After the Fajr Prayer

text: 1446 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «خَمْسَةٌ لاَ يَنَامُونَ اَلْهَامُّ بِدَمٍ يَسْفِكُهُ وَ ذُو اَلْمَالِ اَلْكَثِيرِ لاَ أَمِينَ لَهُ وَ اَلْقَائِلُ فِي اَلنَّاسِ اَلزُّورَ وَ اَلْبُهْتَانَ عَنْ عَرَضٍ مِنَ اَلدُّنْيَا يَنَالُهُ وَ اَلْمَأْخُوذُ بِالْمَالِ اَلْكَثِيرِ وَ لاَ مَالَ لَهُ وَ اَلْمُحِبُّ حَبِيباً يَتَوَقَّعُ فِرَاقَهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1446 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"There are five types of people who cannot sleep: one who is anxious about shedding blood, the possessor of a great amount of wealth who lacks a trustworthy guardian, one who spreads lies and false accusations among people to gain a worldly benefit, the person burdened with a large amount of wealth but has none himself, and one who deeply loves someone and fears separation from them."
